previous_statement: P is real-linear on B(H)_sa. Phi is real-linear (GT-positive-unital), hence so are S=2Phi-I, U=S^2-I, each partial sum sum_{k<=N}C(-1/2,k)U^k, and their norm-limit R (node 1.2). Therefore sgn(S)=S R and P=(I+sgn(S))/2 are real-linear.
new_statement: P is real-linear on B(H)_sa. Phi is real-linear by hypothesis (def positive-unital-map: Phi:B(H)_sa->B(H)_sa real-linear is part of the definition). Hence S=2Phi-I and U=S^2-I are real-linear. Each partial sum sum_{n<=N} a_n U^n is real-linear (real coefficients a_n times compositions of the real-linear U). R is the operator-norm limit of these partial sums (node 1.2); a norm-limit of real-linear maps is real-linear: for real s,t and x,y, R(s x+t y)=lim_N R_N(s x+t y)=lim_N (s R_N x+t R_N y)=s Rx+t Ry by continuity of the vector-space operations. Therefore sgn(S)=S R and P=(I+sgn(S))/2 are real-linear. Uses node 1.2.
